options: It stays open — the exception skips all cleanup
It closes only if you also add a finally block
Java closes it automatically before the exception continues upward
It closes only when the garbage collector eventually runs
mcq.explanation_md: The whole point of try-with-resources: any resource declared in the parentheses is closed **automatically**, success or failure, before the exception travels on. That's why it replaced manual cleanup. The tempting wrong answer is the `finally` one — that was the *old* pattern; with try-with-resources, adding a `finally` for closing is redundant. And waiting for the garbage collector is dangerous thinking: GC frees memory on its own schedule and gives no promise about when (or whether) an open file handle is released.
